title: Cymopterus minimus
taxonomy: Cymopterus minimus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the carrot family, Apiaceae. The genus Cymopterus belongs to the tribe Selineae. This plant is native to North America and is often found in dry areas such as rocky outcrops and open woodlands. Cymopterus minimus is a small, annual or biennial herb that produces white or yellowish flowers in clusters. It is considered a rare or local plant in some regions, adding to its unique classification within the plant kingdom.
uses: Cymopterus minimus, also known as small spring-parsley or small spring centaury, is a plant that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. intro: Cymopterus minimus, also known as the small spring parsley or western spring parsley, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family that is native to the western United States and Canada. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in traditional medicine to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems.
